mnemonics: EVERY Class Is An Object (ECIAO) - Because in Java, every class ultimately inherits from the Object class.
keyPoints: The Object class is the root of the class hierarchy; every class has Object as a superclass.
It provides critical default implementations for methods like toString(), equals(), hashCode(), and getClass().
Understanding and properly overriding these methods (especially equals and hashCode) is fundamental to writing correct Java code.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to override the hashCode() method when overriding equals(), violating the general contract that equal objects must have equal hash codes.
Using the default toString() method for meaningful output, which returns a string of the class name and the object's hash code, often not useful for debugging or logging.
